headline: Red Cards Galore in Mexico and South Korea's Late Show!
theDrama: Mexico and South Africa decided that football was secondary to a proper physical battle, racking up three red cards between them in a chaotic 2-0 win for the hosts. Absolute scenes. Meanwhile, South Korea actually played some football, edging past the Czech Republic 2-1 in a much more civilized affair.
mustWatchHighlights: Mexico vs South Africa was the clear must-watch for the sheer Sunday League defending and chaotic discipline. Avoid Korea vs Czech Republic if you prefer your football with a side of red-card madness.
progressionNews: Mexico and South Korea put their feet up with three points, while South Africa's discipline has left them in a right mess for the rest of the group stage.

headline: Yanks Run Riot While Canada Bottle It on Home Soil!
theDrama: Canada dominated possession but defended like a Sunday League side to draw 1-1 with Bosnia. Meanwhile, the USA put on a clinic at SoFi Stadium, hammering Paraguay 4-1. The Paraguayans spent more time collecting five yellow cards than actually trying to play football.
mustWatchHighlights: USA vs Paraguay was a masterclass in liquid football from the Americans. Skip Canada's frustrating stalemate unless you enjoy watching paint dry.
progressionNews: The USA are flying high and looking like a walking cheat code, while Canada are already facing squeaky bum time if they want to escape the group.

headline: Swiss Bottlers, Brazilian Blunders, and Scotland's Great Escape!
theDrama: Switzerland had 26 shots against Qatar and somehow only managed a 1-1 draw. Absolute bottlers. Brazil looked like they were having a casual kickabout in the park and let Morocco snatch a 1-1 draw. Shockingly, Scotland actually won a football match, scraping a 1-0 win over Haiti.
mustWatchHighlights: Brazil vs Morocco was a proper, high-quality contest. Avoid Qatar vs Switzerland unless you want to watch Swiss strikers miss the broad side of a barn.
progressionNews: Scotland actually have hope! Brazil and Switzerland have left themselves with work to do after some seriously lazy performances.

headline: German Demolition Derby and Turkey's Ultimate Smash-and-Grab Disaster!
theDrama: Turkey had 30 shots and 71% possession against Australia and still lost 2-0. Absolute comedy. Germany treated Curacao like a Sunday League pub team, putting seven past them in a 7-1 slaughter. Sweden also ran riot, hammering Tunisia 5-1.
mustWatchHighlights: Germany vs Curacao for the sheer, unadulterated slaughter, or Netherlands vs Japan for a proper 2-2 thriller. Avoid Ivory Coast vs Ecuador unless you love boring 1-0s.
progressionNews: Germany and Sweden are basically through to the knockouts. Turkey need to find their shooting boots immediately or they will be booking early flights home.

headline: Spain's Passing to Nowhere and Uruguay's Striking Horror Show!
theDrama: Spain had 74% possession and 27 shots against Cape Verde but couldn't score. Classic Spain, passing the opposition to death but forgetting where the goal is. Uruguay did the exact same against Saudi Arabia in another frustrating 1-1 draw.
mustWatchHighlights: Iran vs New Zealand was a cracking 2-2 draw with plenty of action. Avoid Spain vs Cape Verde unless you desperately need a cure for insomnia.
progressionNews: Spain and Uruguay have made life incredibly difficult for themselves, while Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia celebrate historic, hard-fought points.

headline: Haaland Cruises and Argentina Put on a Masterclass!
theDrama: Norway dismantled Iraq 4-1 with Erling Haaland looking like a walking cheat code. Argentina brushed Algeria aside 3-0 without breaking a sweat, playing some lovely liquid football that made the Algerians look like they were chasing shadows.
mustWatchHighlights: Iraq vs Norway to see some proper clinical finishing. Avoid Argentina vs Algeria if you wanted a competitive match, as it was a total mismatch.
progressionNews: France, Norway, and Argentina are cruising towards the knockouts, while Iraq and Algeria look dead and buried already.

headline: England's Four-Goal Fiesta and Portugal's Portuguese Cock-up!
theDrama: England actually looked competent, putting four past Croatia in a 4-2 thriller. Portugal, however, had 75% possession against DR Congo and managed exactly one shot on target in a shocking 1-1 draw. Absolute bottle job from Ronaldo and the lads.
mustWatchHighlights: England vs Croatia for absolute scenes and goals galore. Avoid Portugal vs DR Congo unless you love watching sideways passes in your spare time.
progressionNews: England and Colombia are looking good for the next round. Portugal are in a spot of bother after completely bottling their opening fixture.

headline: Canada's Six-Goal Slaughter and Qatar's Double Red Card Meltdown!
theDrama: Canada absolutely battered Qatar 6-0. Qatar had zero shots on target and two red cards, essentially having a casual kickabout in the park while Canada ran riot. Switzerland also cruised, beating ten-man Bosnia 4-1.
mustWatchHighlights: Canada vs Qatar for the absolute demolition and chaotic red cards. Avoid Czech Republic vs South Africa, which was a drab 1-1 draw.
progressionNews: Canada and Switzerland have put themselves in prime position for the knockouts, while Qatar can start packing their bags after a disastrous display.

headline: Scotland Bottle It Again as Turkey Suffer Another 32-Shot Disaster!
theDrama: Scotland managed zero shots on target and lost 1-0 to Morocco. Classic Scotland. Turkey had 32 shots, 78% possession, and somehow lost 1-0 to ten-man Paraguay. You couldn't write it if you tried!
mustWatchHighlights: Turkey vs Paraguay for the sheer, hilarious injustice of it all. Avoid Scotland vs Morocco unless you enjoy watching Scottish heartbreak.
progressionNews: USA and Brazil are safely through. Scotland are on the brink of booking their flights home, as is tradition for the Tartan Army.

headline: Dutch Demolition, German Drama, and Ecuador's Goal Drought Continues!
theDrama: Today was a mixed bag of emotions! The Netherlands absolutely steamrolled Sweden, putting on a proper goal-scoring masterclass. Germany, on the other hand, had to dig deep, snatching a late winner against a very stubborn Côte d'Ivoire side. And then there was the absolute snooze-fest between Ecuador and Curaçao, where neither side could hit a cow's backside with a banjo, despite Ecuador having enough shots to sink a battleship!
mustWatchHighlights: If you want goals and excitement, the **Netherlands vs Sweden** match is your absolute must-watch. Five goals, end-to-end stuff, proper liquid football! The **Germany vs Côte d'Ivoire** game also had a dramatic late winner, so worth a look for the tension. As for **Ecuador vs Curaçao**? Unless you're a fan of goalkeeping heroics and absolutely no goals, you can probably just watch paint dry instead.
progressionNews: Germany are officially through as Group E winners, looking formidable! The Netherlands are practically there too, topping Group F with seven points and a fantastic goal difference. Sweden and Côte d'Ivoire are both sitting on three points, desperately hoping their goal difference is enough to sneak through as a best third-placed team. Ecuador and Curaçao, both on two points and with Ecuador having zero goals scored, are looking like they've booked their flights home. Hard luck, lads.

headline: World Cup Day of Drama: Japan and Spain Cruise, Belgium Bottle It, and Egypt Fight Back!
theDrama: What a day of football! Japan and Spain put on masterclasses, cruising to comfortable 4-0 victories and looking utterly dominant. Meanwhile, Belgium's 'Golden Generation' completely bottled it, crashing out with a goalless draw and a red card – you just love to see it, don't you? Egypt showed real character to come from behind and secure their spot, while Uruguay and Cape Verde played out a thrilling draw that sadly sent both home.
mustWatchHighlights: If you missed anything, definitely check out Japan's clinical dismantling of Tunisia and Spain's effortless stroll against Saudi Arabia. For pure drama, Egypt's comeback against New Zealand was a cracker. As for Belgium vs. Iran? You can safely put your feet up and skip that snooze-fest, unless you enjoy watching a team with 70% possession fail to score and get a man sent off.
progressionNews: Japan, Spain, Egypt, and Iran have all qualified for the next round, looking strong and ready for the knockouts. Uruguay also scraped through, despite a nervy draw. Sadly, Tunisia, Saudi Arabia, Belgium, Cape Verde, and New Zealand are all eliminated and will be heading home. Better luck next time, lads!
